qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Missing smart-contract guard on staking precompile delegate/undelegate/redelegate/cancelUnbondingDelegation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes incorrect state handling during nested EVM execution paths in the ICS20 precompile (CWE-670), where recursive call state updates are not reflected in the outer execution context. This finding describes a missing smart-contract access control guard on the staking precompile (0x800) — a different component with a different root cause (missing authorization check vs incorrect state management in nested execution).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes incorrect state handling during nested EVM execution paths in the ICS20 precompile of Cosmos EVM (github.com/cosmos/evm), where recursive call state updates are not reflected in the outer execution context, enabling repeated use of the same token balance within a single transaction (CWE-670). The sole qualifying finding describes a missing smart-contract access control guard on the staking precompile — a different precompile with a different root cause (missing authorization check vs incorrect state management in nested execution).
evidence: Advisory component is ICS20 precompile; staking_precompile_access_control_bypass targets the staking precompile (0x800) with a missing authorization check root cause.
Advisory root cause is incorrect state handling during nested EVM execution (CWE-670); finding root cause is missing smart-contract guard on staking methods.
The finding does not reference ICS20 transfer logic, recursive call state management, or the state consistency bug described in the advisory.
